nodes.label: Attribute
nodes.definition: Measurable characteristics of AI systems that, if realized, will ensure they address needs.

nodes.label: Effective
nodes.definition: AI systems that successfully achieve their intended objectives while optimizing resource usage, maintaining high performance, and delivering consistent, reliable result across their designated tasks.

nodes.label: Deployable
nodes.definition: AI systems that are production-ready, scalable, and capable of operating reliably in real-world environments with proper integration into existing infrastructure, monitoring capabilities, and maintainable architecture.

nodes.label: Trustworthy
nodes.definition: AI systems that demonstrate consistent reliability, transparency, and safety while adhering to ethical principles, protecting privacy, maintaining security, and ensuring accountability in their operations and decision-making processes.

nodes.label: Affordable
nodes.definition: Systems can be deployed to meet mission needs within the (likely) available budget (funding and time).

nodes.label: Available
nodes.definition: Systems are operational at the required point in time, under a given set of environmental conditions.

nodes.label: Modular
nodes.definition: Components and systems support System of Systems (SoS) designs where each level and each system is to some extent independent of others. The independent modular systems interoperate with each other through standardized interfaces enabling greater flexibility to promote better evolution of SoS designs.

nodes.label: Adaptable
nodes.definition: The quality of a system that allows it to adjust or be modified for use in different specified environmentsor purposes with minimal external intervention, while maintaining its functional performance and integrity.

nodes.label: Interoperability
nodes.definition: Ability to operate and interact with other system.

nodes.label: Repeatable
nodes.definition: Ensure consistent task performance across varying instances.

nodes.label: Maintainability
nodes.definition: The capacity of an AI system to be updated, repaired, and adapted over time, ensuring long-term reliability and functionality.

nodes.label: Usability
nodes.definition: The ease with which users can interact with and effectively utilize an AI system.

nodes.label: CostEffective
nodes.definition: Evaluates the system's efficiency in achieving its mission objectives relative to the total costs involved

nodes.label: CostFeasible
nodes.definition: Metric assesses whether the financial requirements for development, implementation, maintenance, and operation align with available funding and time resources.

nodes.label: Reinvestment
nodes.definition: Ability to sustainably fund ongoing development, updates, optimizations through cost savings or value generated by the system itself.

nodes.label: Scalability
nodes.definition: Metric evaluates how well the system can accommodate increased demands, whether through handling more data, users, or complex tasks, without requiring increases in resources or costs.

nodes.label: Monitoring
nodes.definition: The continuous assessment of an AI system's performance, health, and operational status through automated processes.

nodes.label: Maintenance
nodes.definition: Process of performing regular updates, repairs, and optimizations.

nodes.label: DeploymentPipelines
nodes.definition: Infrastructure ensures that AI systems can be efficiently moved from development through to production

nodes.label: Scalable
nodes.definition: Metric evaluates how well each module can adapt to changing demands, whether through handling increased workloads, accommodating more users,or processing larger datasets, without compromising the overall system integration.

nodes.label: Flexibility
nodes.definition: Evaluates how effectively individual modules can be modified, replaced, or repurposed without disrupting the broader system's functionality

nodes.label: Upgradability
nodes.definition: Capacity of individual components within a System of Systems (SoS) to be enhanced, updated, or replaced with improved versions

nodes.label: ReputableSources
nodes.definition: System's ability to draw from data that is verified and widely recognized for its reliability and authenticity.

nodes.label: Versatile
nodes.definition: To perform effectively across a diverse range of new use cases without requiring significant modifications.

nodes.label: DataStandards
nodes.definition: Established protocols and formats that ensure consistent data representation, exchange, and processing across different systems and platforms.

nodes.label: Integration
nodes.definition: Ability to connect within existing infrastructures, ensuring effective communication and data exchange.

nodes.label: APICompatibility
nodes.definition: Interact effectively with other systems through standardized Application Programming Interfaces (APIs).

nodes.label: BenchmarkTesting
nodes.definition: Conduct regular tests using predefined benchmarks to confirm that the system continually meets expected performance standards across instances.

nodes.label: Performance
nodes.definition: Monitor any deviations in performance metrics, such as speed or accuracy, when the system is used repeatedly in similar scenarios.

nodes.label: Precision
nodes.definition: Identify relevant instances or information without including irrelevant or incorrect data.

nodes.label: HighRecall
nodes.definition: AI system to identify and capture all or nearly all relevant instances or pieces of information from a given dataset.

nodes.label: CodeQuality
nodes.definition: Codebase is well-structured, clear, and efficient, promoting ease of understanding and modification.

nodes.label: Documentation
nodes.definition: Quality of documents, such as user manuals, developer guides, and API references, ensuring they are comprehensive and easy to understand.

nodes.label: Support
nodes.definition: Provide assistance to ensure smooth operation, troubleshooting, and updates.

nodes.label: Responsiveness
nodes.definition: The speed and efficiency with which an AI system reacts to user inputs or commands, providing timely feedback and interaction

nodes.label: Visuals
nodes.definition: Layout ontology or graph with color scheme to visually see a type of hierarchy to ensure they facilitate clear communication.

nodes.label: MultiModel
nodes.definition: Support multiple forms of input and interaction modes,  such as text, images, audio, and other sensory information.

nodes.label: Universal
nodes.definition: Inputs and outputs are treated in a universal way to enable cross-modality learning.

nodes.label: Accurate
nodes.definition: A measure of an AI system's performance that reflects its capacity to produce outputs that align closely with verified data or truths.

nodes.label: Reliable
nodes.definition: Explicit, well-defined uses are documented, and the safety, security, and effectiveness of such capabilities are subject to testing and assurance within those defined uses across their entire life-cycles.

nodes.label: Generalizable
nodes.definition: Predictions are relevant to out of domain or shifted data distributions.

nodes.label: Insightful
nodes.definition: Robust semantic understanding of modalities and interactions, leading to effective designs.

nodes.label: Comprehensive
nodes.definition: Signifies its ability to thoroughly address or encompass a wide range of scenarios, inputs, and potential outcomes.

nodes.label: CrossModality
nodes.definition: Concerned with the development of algorithms that can learn from multiple modalities of data.

nodes.label: SpecializedModels
nodes.definition: Ability to make specialized models.

nodes.label: HighQuality
nodes.definition: High Quality refers curating the datasets and writing the best code.

nodes.label: SummarizingData
nodes.definition: A process within an AI system that involves distilling large volumes of information into a concise and coherent representation.

nodes.label: LogicalReasoning
nodes.definition: Apply structured thinking and inferential processes to arrive at conclusions or predictions that are consistent with established facts and principles.

nodes.label: ComputationAvailability
nodes.definition: Evaluates the system's ability to consistently access necessary processing power and memory, ensuring that tasks are executed efficiently and without interruption

nodes.label: HumanAiTeaming
nodes.definition: Productive collaboration between human users and AI systems, enabling both parties to complement each other's strengths in achieving common objectives.

nodes.label: Learning
nodes.definition: System's ability to effectively transfer knowledge from observed to unobserved scenarios, drawing insights from diverse data to enhance its capability to generalize.

nodes.label: Predictions
nodes.definition: Generate accurate and relevant estimations that remain valid under diverse conditions ensuring that the predictions are consistently reliable across a wide range of scenarios.

nodes.label: Regularization
nodes.definition: System's use of regularization methods to reduce overfitting, promoting a model that generalizes well across diverse and unseen datasets.

nodes.label: DiverseData
nodes.definition: Specifically refers to havimg diverse training data. Helps ensure that it learns to generalize rather than overfitting to specific data.

nodes.label: ModelCards
nodes.definition: Structured documentation that provides detailed information about an AI model's characteristics, intended use cases, performance metrics, and limitations.

nodes.label: Ideation
nodes.definition: Strategic process of generating, developing, and refining new concepts and approaches.

nodes.label: UnderstandModalities
nodes.definition: The capability to grasp and interpret various forms of data inputs and their interactions, such as text, images, audio, and other sensory information.

nodes.label: MissionOriented
nodes.definition: The alignment and focus of an AI system's functionalities and goals toward achieving specific, predefined objectives or purposes.

nodes.label: EffectiveDesign
nodes.definition: Ensuring that the design aligns with the users' needs and mission-oriented goals.

nodes.label: LowRecall
nodes.definition: Scenarios where the system retrieves fewer relevant instances or data points from the total available, possibly due to prioritizing precision over recall.

nodes.label: TradeOffs
nodes.definition: Management of competing priorities or constraints, which may require balancing different aspects such as precision, recall, computational resources, and accuracy.

nodes.label: BalancePrecisionRecall
nodes.definition: Effectively manage between precision and recall to achieve an optimal balance that suits the task's objectives.

nodes.label: Safe
nodes.definition: Quality of an AI system to operate without causing harm or unintended consequences to users.

nodes.label: Secure
nodes.definition: This trait focuses on the team's commitment to incorporating robust security protocols, maintaining data protection standards, and following cybersecurity best practices during development and deployment.

nodes.label: Ethical
nodes.definition: The commitment to developing and deploying AI systems in accordance with moral principles and responsible practices.

nodes.label: Explainable
nodes.definition: The ability of an AI system to provide clear, understandable explanations of the system's decision-making processes and outputs.

nodes.label: Transparency
nodes.definition: The clarity in all aspects of the AI system's operations, including its data usage, decision-making processes, and limitations.This trait ensures that stakeholders have appropriate visibility into how the system functions, what data it uses, and how it arrives at its conclusions.

nodes.label: Robustness
nodes.definition: The ability of an AI system to maintain consistent and reliable performance across varying conditions, inputs, and potential adversarial scenarios.

nodes.label: QuantifiedUncertainty
nodes.definition: Employs systematic modality-aware uncertainty quantification.

nodes.label: Accountability
nodes.definition: Trait encompasses mechanisms for tracking, auditing, and attributing system behaviors to specific components, processes, or human oversight.

nodes.label: SystemStability
nodes.definition: Maintain consistent, reliable, and predictable performance under various operating conditions without experiencing failures or unexpected behavior.

nodes.label: RiskAssessment
nodes.definition: Process of identifying, evaluating, and mitigating potential hazards and vulnerabilities that could compromise the safe operation

nodes.label: CyberSecurityMeasures
nodes.definition: The comprehensive set of practices, protocols, and tools implemented by development teams to protect AI systems from cyber threats and vulnerabilities.

nodes.label: MissionDependent
nodes.definition: The alignment of specific mission objectives while maintaining appropriate ethical considerations and constraints.

nodes.label: ProtectsPrivacy
nodes.definition: The systematic implementation of data protection mechanisms, anonymization techniques, and user consent management to safeguard personal and sensitive information throughout the AI system's lifecycle.

nodes.label: ProtectsCivilRights
nodes.definition: The dedication to ensuring equal treatment and prevention of discrimination through comprehensive bias detection, mitigation strategies, and fair representation of data across diverse populations.

nodes.label: ProtectsCivilLiberties
nodes.definition: The commitment to preserving and respecting fundamental freedoms through fair decision-making processes, recognition of religious practices, and protection of individual privacy rights.

nodes.label: OutputReasoning
nodes.definition: Clearly articulate and justify the logic factors that led to specific decisions or results.

nodes.label: Understandble
nodes.definition: Comprehensible to its intended audience.

nodes.label: ReflectsPurpose
nodes.definition: The alignment between explanations and its intended functional objectives, ensuring that the provided insights directly relate to core purpose.

nodes.label: DataDiscovery
nodes.definition: The capability to identify, track, and reveal the sources and characteristics of data used in the AI system's decision-making processes

nodes.label: ExplanationOfDecisions
nodes.definition: Understand the key factors, logic, and data that influenced specific decisions made by AI system.

nodes.label: ModelInterpretability
nodes.definition: The degree to which an AI system's internal workings, decision-making processes, and feature interactions can be understood and analyzed by humans.

nodes.label: ErrorHandling
nodes.definition: The systematic approach to detecting, managing, responding to errors, exceptions, and unexpected situations that may occur during the AI system's operation.

nodes.label: Reliability
nodes.definition: The consistent ability of an AI system to perform its intended functions accurately and dependably under specified conditions over time.

nodes.label: ResilientToChanges
nodes.definition: System can appropriately respond to various changes, whether they are gradual shifts in data distributions, updates to connected systems, or alterations in operational parameters.

nodes.label: FaultTolerant
nodes.definition: Continue operating effectively even when components fail or errors occur.

nodes.label: DisasterRecoveryPlans
nodes.definition: AI has well-defined processes for data backup, system restoration, and service continuity in the face of major incidents.

nodes.label: VersionControl
nodes.definition: Track AI system to make sure modifications are documented, reversible, traceable, enabling efficient collaboration, problem diagnosis, and system restoration when needed.

nodes.label: Authenitcation
nodes.definition: Security measure to verify and validate the identity of users, systems, and data sources interacting with the AI system.

nodes.label: WaterMarks
nodes.definition: The integration of distinct, identifiable markers or signatures within the AI system's outputs or data to verify authenticity.

nodes.label: EstimationOfUncertainties
nodes.definition: The systematic process of calculatingthe degree of confidence or variability in the AI system's predictions, decisions, and outputs across different modalitiesThis trait involves the use of statistical methods and uncertainty quantification techniques to provide meaningful measures of reliability and potential error rangesin system results. By implementing comprehensive uncertainty estimation, the system enhances its trustworthiness through transparent communication of confidence levels.

nodes.label: Useful
nodes.definition: The practical applicability of uncertainty measurements in supporting decision-making and system operations. This trait evaluates whether the quantified uncertaintiesprovide meaningful, actionable insights that help stakeholders make informed decisions about system outputs and their reliability.

nodes.label: ResponsibleParties
nodes.definition: The clear identification of individuals, teams, or entities who hold specific responsibilities and oversight roles in the AI system's development, deployment, and operation.

nodes.label: Protocols
nodes.definition: The established set of procedures, guidelines, and standardized processes that govern the operations of the AI system.

nodes.label: Compliance
nodes.definition: The adherence to relevant laws, regulations, standards, and ethical guidelines that govern AI system development and deployment.
